title: 'You can complain, nothing will happen': Who protects you when your ride goes wrong?
content: Passengers face challenges with ride-hailing app complaint systems.  Many incidents involve AI chatbots and a lack of human support.  Commuters report issues like driver misbehavior and privacy invasions.  App aggregators claim a two-tier system handles serious safety concerns.  New government initiatives aim to enhance passenger safety during rides.

title: Ronaldo's World Cup ends with painful knockout record: Just one goal in 10 matches
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup journey concluded after Portugal's loss to Spain.  He confirmed that the 2026 tournament would be his final appearance.  Ronaldo leaves behind numerous records and international silverware for Portugal.  His World Cup knockout round statistics showed only one goal and no assists.  The legendary forward expressed sadness but also a clear conscience about his career.

title: Ayodhya cops eye narco, polygraph tests in Ram Temple donation theft probe
content: Ayodhya Police are considering narco-analysis and polygraph tests for five jailed accused. These examinations aim to unravel a larger conspiracy and establish the money trail. The police secured court permission to interrogate the accused inside the district jail. Investigators believe these tests will resolve statement contradictions and trace siphoned funds. The probe also examines institutional lapses and senior functionaries' potential involvement in the embezzlement.

title: World's largest asset management firm CEO worried about Mamdani running New York
content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink voiced concerns about New York City's business climate. He noted that 47% of city tax revenue comes from the top 1% of taxpayers. Fink stated that inadequate services do not justify the current tax burden for residents. He believes economic growth should be prioritized over tax policy by policymakers. Fink suggested that BlackRock might deploy US resources elsewhere if conditions weaken.

title: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's hightest honour during his 2-day state visit
content: The Bintang Republik Indonesia Adipurna, commonly referred to as the Bintang Adipurna, is Indonesia's highest state honour. It is the highest class of the Star of the Republic of Indonesia (Bintang Republik Indonesia), the country's premier order of merit, and is conferred by the President of Indonesia on individuals who have rendered extraordinary service to the nation.

title: 'I want to make request as a father': Neymar's dad pens emotional message
content: Neymar has retired from international football after Brazil's World Cup elimination. His father made an emotional plea for him to continue playing the sport. Neymar confirmed his international career was over following the team's defeat to Norway. He finished as Brazil's all-time leading goalscorer with eighty goals. His father hopes he will continue playing club football.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: China's ballistic missile test: How India can defend against such an attack — and strike back
content: China recently fired a submarine-launched ballistic missile during a joint exercise with Russia. India is actively building a robust Ballistic Missile Defence system with Israeli collaboration. DRDO has tested interceptors designed to counter faster and longer-range missiles. India's indigenous missile submarines are being developed for retaliatory nuclear strikes. Work is progressing on advanced interceptors targeting hypersonic and MIRV-equipped missiles.

title: Typhoon Maysak breaks China dam, kills 2, forces thousands to evacuate
content: Typhoon Maysak caused severe flooding and two deaths in China's Guangxi region. Thousands of residents were evacuated after extensive rainfall and reservoir emergencies. A dam breach sent a massive torrent of water downstream, impacting villages. Rescue efforts utilized drones to deliver supplies and evacuate stranded individuals. Relief funding and supplies have been allocated to support affected provinces.

title: Stories behind capital’s Ozempic shaadis: ‘How did this guy get a girl like you?’
content: A growing number of Indians are turning to GLP-1 injections like Ozempic for rapid weight loss, primarily driven by wedding pressures and social media ideals. While these drugs are effective for obesity, medical professionals express concern over their cosmetic use, potential side effects, and the risk of counterfeit products. Experts warn against short-term use for quick results, emphasizing the need for medical supervision and a holistic approach to weight management.

title: Diljit's 'Satluj' released without certification process being completed: Centre
content: Based on the life of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, Satluj had been at the centre of a prolonged certification dispute before it was released on ZEE5 on Friday under a new title. The streaming platform later withdrew the film from India, saying it would remain unavailable until further notice. According to officials in the information and broadcasting ministry, the film did not have the mandatory certification required for a theatrical release before it was made available online.

title: Wayanad landslide: 5 injured as debris crashes onto tunnel project site amid heavy rain
content: A significant landslide occurred in Wayanad, Kerala, near a tunnel construction site. Local residents rescued three individuals from the debris after the incident. Five workers sustained injuries and are receiving medical attention at a hospital. Rescue operations are underway with police and NDRF teams deployed. Officials are coordinating efforts to ascertain if more people are trapped.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: What sparked Kylian Mbappe-Celeste Amarilla World Cup controversy?
content: France’s World Cup win over Paraguay was overshadowed by a racism controversy involving Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla. After a tense post-match incident between Kylian Mbappe and Orlando Gill, Amarilla posted offensive remarks about the France star on X. Mbappe responded publicly, condemning the comments and defending Paraguay’s players while calling out racism and hatred.

title: Astra missiles, BrahMos & Sabang port: India, Indonesia sign big defence, cooperation deals
content: India will supply BrahMos cruise missiles and Astra air-to-air missiles to Indonesia. Prime Minister Modi's visit aims to strengthen strategic ties and defense cooperation. The countries will also collaborate on critical minerals and maritime connectivity projects. India will assist Indonesia in developing electronic voting machines for elections. This visit marks a significant step in the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.

title: 'You despise me because I am a woman': Paraguay senator fires back at Mbappé
content: Paraguayan Senator Celeste Amarilla seeks an apology from French footballer Kylian Mbappé.  She demands he retract his "despicable woman" comment made after her remarks.  Amarilla claims Mbappé showed disrespect and poor sportsmanship during their World Cup match.  She is considering legal action if Mbappé fails to apologize publicly.  The senator asserts her position as an elected official representing her nation.

title: Rent hike during lease: Can your landlord suddenly increase rent?
content: In India, tenants often encounter steep rent hikes and the looming threat of eviction, primarily due to ambiguous legal standing. The rights and protections available to tenants hinge on their tenancy agreements and the laws of their state. Establishing a written contract shields tenants from arbitrary increases in rent and associated complications, whereas a lack thereof defaults to month-to-month tenancy.

title: Decoding the selection call that ended Samson's T20 WC high - and the road ahead
content: India's T20I squad overhaul has raised fresh questions after Player of the Tournament Sanju Samson was dropped from the Zimbabwe series despite his World Cup heroics, with selectors prioritising teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Meanwhile, discussions are underway over whether Gautam Gambhir and the regular support staff will travel to Zimbabwe due to the quick turnaround, while last-minute changes to India's Asian Games squad also remain on the cards.

title: Gold price prediction today: Will prices rise in the near-term? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold is likely to move higher and consolidate with a bullish bias in the near-term, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Kriti Sanon reveals she had her eggs frozen while filming ‘Mimi’
content: Actress Kriti Sanon shared her empowering decision to freeze her eggs, a choice made during the preparation for 'Mimi' amidst weight gain. She firmly believes that individuals should marry and start families only when they feel genuinely prepared, urging others to resist societal pressures. This choice underscores her pursuit of autonomy in shaping her future family journey.

title: IIM campus, UPI, AI & more: PM Modi heralds 'golden chapter' of India, Indonesia ties
content: India and Indonesia are enhancing cooperation in defense and maritime security.  Discussions covered trade, energy, and critical minerals for future growth.  Both nations agreed to boost technology sharing and startup collaborations.  Educational ties will strengthen with an Indian Institute of Management campus opening.  Cultural exchanges and heritage projects will also be jointly undertaken.

title: Delhi hit-and-run: Speeding Mercedes kills scooty rider near Jahangirpuri; driver arrested
content: A 29-year-old man died after his scooty was hit by a speeding Mercedes. The accident occurred near Jahangirpuri Bus Stand on Delhi's Outer Ring Road. Police arrested the luxury car's driver after tracing the vehicle. The victim was found critically injured and declared brought dead at the hospital. An FIR was registered, and further investigation is currently underway.

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts case: Gujarat high court upholds death for 38, life term for 11
content: The Gujarat High Court upheld a special court's verdict in the 2008 serial blasts case. Thirty-eight individuals received the death sentence, and eleven others were awarded life imprisonment. The court also ordered the state government to provide compensation to victims of the blasts. Compensation amounts were specified for deaths and various injury types. Payments are mandated to be completed by March 31, 2027.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo sets unwanted World Cup record, becomes player with most...
content: At his last World Cup, Cristiano Ronaldo made headlines by attempting numerous shots yet shockingly, none translated into goal-scoring opportunities, establishing a record for missed chances. While his playmaking seemed to deteriorate, he still managed to net three goals from seventeen attempts. Ultimately, Portugal's journey ended prematurely with their exit in the Round of 16.

title: Japan, China clash in high-stakes maritime standoff near disputed islands
content: Japan's coast guard expelled Chinese vessels from waters near disputed islands. China stated its coast guard warned and expelled an intruding Japanese fishing boat. The confrontation occurred amid already strained ties between the two Asian powers. The Senkaku-Diaoyu islands remain a major flashpoint for decades. The East China Sea region is strategically important due to proximity to Taiwan.

title: Gas buys to be doubled from US - how much can it help India cut reliance on Gulf supply?
content: Energy imports from the US have gained importance, particularly following the West Asia conflict, helping reduce the trade imbalance between the two countries. Besides the US, India has also sourced LPG from Argentina, Nigeria and Malaysia. Data from Kpler shows that India’s LPG imports from America have grown substantially since the US-Iran war began. After a marginal dip in April, the supplies have steadily climbed.

title: US, allies rattled after China's missile test - why the launch set off alarm bells
content: China's recent submarine missile test in the Pacific drew sharp reactions from the United States and regional nations. The United States accused Beijing of rapidly expanding its nuclear arsenal and urged arms control talks. Several Indo-Pacific countries, including the Philippines and Taiwan, described the launch as destabilizing and provocative. Analysts view the test as a significant milestone in China's growing sea-based nuclear deterrent capability.

title: Mahindra car developed engine trouble within months; owner wins Rs 1 lakh and new engine
content: The National Consumer Commission ordered Mahindra to replace a Marazzo engine free of cost. The company and its dealer must also pay the customer one lakh rupees in compensation. This decision came after their claim about bad fuel causing damage was found untrue. The commission noted inconsistencies in the dealer's fuel test report and its timing. Lower courts' decisions were set aside due to flawed analysis of evidence.

title: 'Tied up, tortured': UP man claims wife held him captive, gave electric shocks
content: A woman has been taken into custody after her husband alleged that she tied him up, held him captive and assaulted him, including giving him electric shocks using live wires, in Uttar Pradesh's Fatehpur district, police said on Tuesday.

title: The SpudCell: Could some 'blobs' in a dish spark the 'Sputnik' moment for synthetic life?
content: In a remarkable scientific breakthrough, University of Minnesota researchers unveiled SpudCell, a synthetic construct engineered from chemicals to replicate key life processes such as consumption, growth, genetic replication, and division. While it is not classified as living, this innovative achievement in synthetic biology represents a pivotal advancement, prompting exploration into the core principles of life and the prospective engineering of biological systems for diverse applications.

title: Explained: The 12 reasons why Mumbai floods year after year
content: Mumbai faces annual flooding due to extreme rainfall and its low-lying coastal geography. High tides impede stormwater drainage, while aging infrastructure struggles with increased rainfall intensity. Natural buffers like mangroves have diminished, and urban growth strains existing systems. Blocked drains and ongoing construction projects further exacerbate the flooding issues. Climate change is expected to worsen these challenges, requiring long-term planning.

title: After functioning 6 years on paper, Indore’s ‘ghost’ hospital finally sees some hope
content: A proposed 100-bed Khajrana civil hospital officially exists only on paper for six years.  Land for the facility has not been transferred to the health department yet.  No administrative approvals or construction funds have been received for the hospital.  Sanctioned staff were deployed elsewhere as the hospital remains unbuilt.  District collector assured suitable land identification and funding requests will be made.

title: 'Overturn this': Belgium's savage post after crushing USA to reach WC quarter-finals
content: Belgium defeated the United States 4-1 in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.  The Belgian Red Devils posted social media messages after their emphatic victory.  One post sarcastically referenced Folarin Balogun's overturned suspension.  Another post humorously addressed the football versus soccer debate.  This loss ended the United States' World Cup campaign.

title: Rupee recovers 15 paise to 95.28 against US dollar in early trade
content: The Indian rupee opened stronger against the US dollar on Tuesday. Lower crude oil price expectations and easing Middle East tensions improved market sentiment. Saudi Arabia's decision to cut oil prices for Asian buyers added to the positive mood. Foreign institutional investors remained net buyers of domestic equities on Monday. Indian equities also opened on a positive note, with major indices rising.

title: PM in Indonesia LIVE: Modi hails new 'golden chapter in India and Indonesia bilateral relations'
content: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour ’Bintang Adipurna of the Republic of Indonesia’ medal

title: Drunken brawl leads to chopping down of two fully grown trees in Hyderabad
content: The trees in question — a 40-45 years old peepal,  and a 20-25 years old jamun — were in the middle of 40-feet road in RTC Colony, Chintalkunta in the Malkajgiri Municipal Corporation’s purview

title: India, Indonesia expand defence ties; New Delhi to supply BrahMos missiles
content: The BrahMos missile deal with Indonesia came after India inked similar pacts with Vietnam and the Philippines

title: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour 'Bintang Adipurna' in Jakarta
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that the award is a "tribute to the India-Indonesia friendship."

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts: Gujarat High Court upholds death sentence for 38, life term for 11 convicts
content: On July 26, 2008, a series of 21 bomb blasts ripped through Ahmedabad in a span of 70 minutes, killing 56 persons and injuring 200 others

title: At least three children killed, two injured as truck runs over roadside drain cover in Jaipur
content: Truck broke through an iron railing before running over the drain cover on which the five children were standing

title: Lokesh seeks EV, semiconductor investment from Korean giants for Andhra Pradesh
content: On his South Korea visit, the Minister invited Hyundai Mobis, LG Electronics and APACT to set up EV, semiconductor and electronics units; LG confirmed a ₹5,000-crore plant at Sri City

title: Indian kitchens are changing. But are women still doing most of the work?
content: Men are spending more time in kitchens than ever before. But women still shoulder most of the work. We speak to people across the country about gender, labour, and who gets credit.

title: Assam seeks deeper Japan ties in semiconductors, clean energy
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma said Assam is keen to play a greater role in advancing the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership and reaffirmed the State’s commitment to contributing to the growing relationship between the two countries

title: Bengaluru special court rejects Priyank Kharge’s claim that RSS member can’t file defamation case as it is not a registered organisation
content: Various courts have declared that ‘the RSS is a definite, determinate, and identifiable body or class of persons; and consequently, where defamatory imputations are made against the RSS as an organisation, an individual member of the RSS is competent to maintain a complaint for defamation’

title: NCW seeks Action Taken Report from Bengal DGP on Baruipur minor's rape-murder
content: "The Commission has sought details regarding the registration of the FIR under the appropriate provisions of the BNS and the POCSO Act, the immediate arrest of all persons involved in the alleged rape and murder, and the progress of the investigation," says Commission statement

title: U.S. technology company’s move to shut down centres in Kerala taking down around 800 jobs halted
content: Employees in Kochi and Kozhikode offices of the firm CorroHealth Infotech Private Limited were terminated reportedly without prior notice

title: Karnataka rains: Schools closed in parts of Malnad region
content: Primary schools, high schools and anganwadi centres in Koppa, Sringeri and Mudigere taluks of Chikkamagaluru district remained closed on July 7

title: TVK man threatens  VB-G RAM G workers; BJP wants Vijay to control his partymen
content: Mr. Nagenthran further urged Mr. Vijay to control his partymen from interfering in the functioning of the State-run schools, goverment hospitals and employment programmes.

title: Andhra fishermen rescued off Odisha’s Puri, Paradip; six still missing off Visakhapatnam: Atchannaidu
content: Ten fishermen were rescued off Puri and another group has reached Paradip, the Minister said; the search continued for six others still missing off Visakhapatnam

title: NSCN-IM denies role in Manipur attack, says committed to peace process
content: NSCN-IM condemns all actions detrimental to the peace process and the political talks, urging stakeholders to exercise restraint and responsibility

title: Street vendors in Bengaluru will be relocated, not displaced: Karnataka Chief Minister Shivakumar
content: Chief Minister says registered vendors will be provided pushcarts and alternative vending spaces; promises no shortage of funds for New Anubhava Mantapa project at Basavakalyan

title: Caste Census: The methodology, challenges, and the road ahead | Explained
content: After decades of debate, caste will be counted as part of the 2027 Census. From the ongoing pre-test and questionnaire design to past anomalies and the road ahead, here’s what you need to know

title: Messi, Qatar and a mailbox in Kozhikode: The story of a 500-piece FIFA World Cup stamp collection
content: ​For more than three decades, a Kozhikode philatelist has documented FIFA World Cup history through philately, building a collection of nearly 500 commemorative stamps

title: Flash floods hit J&K’s Doda; several structures, vehicles suffer damage
content: Officials said heavy rain in the upper reaches triggered flash floods, bringing boulders, mud and debris into Thathri town

title: Telangana Fire Department launches civil defence volunteer training at two universities
content: The volunteers to received training in rescue operations, firefighting, first aid, crowd management, disaster preparedness, community awareness and relief distribution

title: U.P. govt. committed to ensuring that every needy receives benefits of welfare schemes, says CM Yogi
content: Mr. Adityanath held Janata Darshan in Lucknow and met every petitioner who had come from different districts of the State and listened to their grievances

title: Anbumani, PMK MLAs meet WRD Minister N. Anand
content: Dr. Anbumani urged the State government to implement 12 water management projects, and said around ₹1 lakh crore should be invested in water projects over the next 10 years despite the financial burden experienced by the State

title: Rogue elephant sparks safety concerns along Chittoor-Bengaluru highway
content: Forest Department has intensified field-level mitigation measures; DFO appeals to the public not to approach, provoke, or disturb the elephant and to immediately report its movement to the forest officials.

title: Faith knows no age as 116-year-old completes Tirumala trek
content: Centenarian devotee’s foot pilgrimage goes viral, prompting TTD to trace her and facilitate a special darshan; Chief Minister praises her devotion

title: ₹17-cr. Rain Forest project planned to promote eco-tourism in Amaravati
content: ADCL invites bids for the project, envisaged as an eco-tourism destination blending biodiversity conservation, environmental education and nature-based recreation

title: Modi begins three-nation trip, starts with Indonesia to boost strategic ties
content: Prime Minister is set to hold discussions with Indonesian President Subianto to add ‘momentum to this partnership across diverse sectors’; he will travel to Australia and New Zealand next as part of efforts to promote a free and open Indo-Pacific

title: PM Narendra Modi receives grand ceremonial welcome in Jakarta
content: The Prime Minister had arrived in Indonesia on July 6, launching the opening segment of his three-nation diplomatic visit with a high-profile ceremonial welcome that featured an escort by an Indonesian Air Force fighter jet and a personal reception by the Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto at the airport

title: Reviving the lost Patna Kalam: How Bihar is bringing back the lost art that captured everyday India
content: Bihar is seeing renewed interest in the nearly forgotten 18th-century art form, in which Mughal miniature met European naturalism

title: Day after M.P. CM inaugurates village road; portion washed away in rain
content: Videos of the damaged portions have gone viral on the internet with locals alleging negligence on part of officials and the construction agency

title: Watch: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India | Explained
content: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

title: In a first, Gujarat building two air-filled rubber dams using South Korean technology
content: The two projects are being built across the Heran River at Rajvasana village in Chhota Udepur district and the Ambika River at Pathakwadi village in Tapi district at a combined cost of more than ₹162 crore

title: Cloud seeding begins in Karnataka as six MLAs fund exercise in Haveri district
content: In view of the scanty monsoon this season, six MLAs from the district have come together to extend financial assistance to make rain

title: The Delimitation Debate: The Union and its Units
content: This e-book by The Hindu frames the upcoming delimitation not merely as a technical administrative adjustment, but as a critical re-litigation of India’s organising principles and its federal compact.

title: Delhi HC protects actor Ravi Kishan’s personality rights, restrains unauthorised use of traits
content: The order was passed on July 2 in a suit filed by the actor alleging widespread misuse of his identity across social media and websites with AI-generated content

title: Hill Palace police register FIR on actor Ansiba Hassan’s complaint; legal team alleges attempt to shield accused
content: Ms. Hassan’s legal team has expressed dissatisfaction with the way the case has been framed, alleging obfuscation of facts to shield the accused

title: Why Pakistan-based Hafiz Saeed is wanted in India? | Explained
content: Why is Saeed so important for India? What charges is Saeed facing? Where is the LeT chief operating from?

title: Kozhikode City police expand drone surveillance over newly identified drug hotspots
content: The initiative is expected to strengthen intelligence gathering and improve the police squads’ ability to detect illegal activities in difficult-to-access areas

title: Bihar NDA leaders differ on alleged police encounter of youth Bharat Bhushan Tiwari
content: Bharat Bhushan Tiwari (28) a resident of Bilauti village of Bhojpur district in Bihar was killed in an alleged police encounter on June 17 at his village

title: About 100 people from Karnataka in attendance at Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral in Tehran
content: Many attendees, including students and professionals already based in Iran, traveled on a chartered flight from Bengaluru, drawn by a deep personal connection to Khamenei, who had previously visited their village.

title: POCSO pendency and torturous process further victimise children subjected to sexual abuse
content: A staggering 18,733 POCSO cases are pending in various courts across the 38 districts in Tamil Nadu. The situation is exacerbated by patchy support services for children who opt to go through the procedural justice system

title: Chief Minister Joseph Vijay writes to Prime Minister Narendra Modi opposing proposed amendment to Food Security Act
content: In letter to PM, he urges the Centre to retain the existing entitlement of 35 kg of foodgrains per month for every household covered under the Antyodaya Anna Yojana, irrespective of family size

title: Chief Minister Vijay visits desalination plants and drinks water
content: An official release said that Mr. Vijay directed officials to ensure proper maintenance of the Nemmeli desalination plants so that drinking water is supplied to the public without interruption

title: Three held for Baruipur rape and murder; govt. will ensure capital punishment is awarded, says CM
content: There may be a communal angle to the lynching of a man in the unrest after the incident, Suvendu Adhikari says; police form a SIT, register four cases

title: WhatsApp gets more time to reply to username notice, assures no India rollout till talks end
content: Meta-owned WhatsApp has been granted a three-day extension to respond to a government notice regarding its controversial username feature, which allows users to communicate without sharing phone numbers.

title: Form used in rehearsal for second phase of Census has an ‘open column’ to record caste
content: Final methodology to enumerate caste as part of Census 2027 will be prepared based on the feedback received during the test exercise; the open column methodology had resulted in the 2011 caste survey returning over 46 lakh different ‘caste names’

title: Controversial book: police search Samagra Shiksha office in Jammu
content: Action part of an ongoing investigation to identify people responsible for the book Great Personalities and Legends of J&K, which has profiled separatist leaders such as Maqbool Bhat, Masarat Alam, Shabir Shah and Syed Ali Geelani

title: Odisha SIR: Malkangiri records 27,653 deletions, highest in the State
content: Ganjam, which sends the highest number of migrant labourers to different parts of country and outside, has the highest deletion of 2,07,626 voters followed by Cuttack district with 1,55,164.

title: Video: College Students Cross Barricade, Get Trapped At Karnataka Waterfall
content: The incident took place at Mallalli Falls after the students allegedly crossed a safety barricade and entered a restricted area near the middle of the waterfall.

title: Bengal Teen's Throat Slit In Revenge Killing Over Football Match
content: Three men attacked Prosenjit with sharp weapons and slit his throat in Baruipur in South 24 Paraganas district bordering Kolkata.

title: "You Want Us To Regulate Chief Minister's Visits?" Supreme Court Rap For DMK
content: Supreme Court criticised DMK for seeking restrictions on Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Joseph Vijay's speeches in relation to the Karur stampede case.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Vladimir Putin Hasn't Looked This Desperate In Over 25 Years
content: As the Russian position weakens, there is possibility of a coup against Putin, for whom just too many things have gone wrong in the last four years.

title: The Tale of Two Monsoons: Heavy Rains In Mumbai, Dry Spell In Delhi
content: The experts argue that this distinction between showers received between Delhi and Mumbai has always existed.

title: Astra Missiles, BrahMos Battery Defence Deals In PM Modi's Indonesia Visit
content: In a big endorsement of India's election management model, India will also support the development of Indonesia-specific EVMs, sources said.

title: Florida Influencer DreamDoll Brii, 21, Killed While Driving Her Lamborghini
content: The 21-year-old influencer, whose real name was Brianna Johnson, died after a shooting in Miramar, Florida.

title: Trump's Friend Urges Him To Declare "National Emergency" Ahead Of Midterms
content: Ticktin, who says he has been friends with Trump since their days at the New York Military Academy, believes that there could be foreign intelligence through electronic voting machines.

title: Pakistan's "War" Threat To India Over Indus Waters Amid Monsoon Blues
content: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari also warned that Pakistan was prepared to fight "on all fronts" over the Indus waters.

title: Ex-Chinese Official Sentenced To Death For Taking $325 Million In Bribes Over 30 Years
content: China sentenced former official Yang Youlin to death for taking $325M in bribes over 3 decades, marking a major escalation in its anti-graft drive.

title: Tamil Nadu Man, 24, Arrested For Sexually Assaulting Woman, 50, Near Park
content: A 24-year-old man is arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting a woman in Tamil Nadu's Tirupathur district.

title: US Man Sues ICE, Says Home Visit Over Email Violated Free Speech Rights
content: David Streever sued ICE after agents targeted his home over an email criticizing leadership, sparking a major First Amendment legal battle.

title: Over 300 Children Killed Or Wounded In Sudan War Over 6 Months: UNICEF
content: UNICEF reports over 300 children killed or wounded in Sudan's civil war over six months, with drone strikes causing 60% of casualties nationwide

title: Storms Kill 8 In China As Typhoon Bavi Looms Offshore
content: At least eight dead as thunderstorms with 149 kph winds batter Hubei Province; torrential rain and Super Typhoon Bavi threaten millions across China

title: US Cybersecurity Agency Uses Anthropic AI Model To Audit Government Software
content: CISA is using Anthropic's Mythos AI to scan government code repositories for security bugs, uncovering many vulnerabilities amid ongoing tensions

title: 11-Year-Old Last Seen In CCTV Footage Before Bengal Rape-Murder Horror
content: The CCTV footage appears to show the 11-year-old girl shortly before she went missing on Saturday. Her body was later found stuffed in a sack.
